purpose: Complete frontend rebuild reference for LLM coding agents. Keeps every real route, component, API contract, DB column, and data-flow from the current app intact, and replaces the visual/interaction layer with a calm, minimal, premium-cooperative aesthetic — no gauges, no glow, no scanlines. Clean over clever.
designThesis: This is the platform a well-run dairy cooperative would actually put its name on: quiet, confident, legible from across a room, and built to be trusted by an inspector as much as an operator. One accent color, one typeface family, generous whitespace, and a single recurring motif (the seal ring) instead of a cabinet of instrument metaphors.
signatureElement: The Seal Ring — a flat, thin-stroke circular progress ring (no needle, no tick marks, no gradient) used for compliance score, sensor health, and per-employee compliance. It reads like the quality stamp on a dairy product, not a dashboard widget.

techStack.styling: Vanilla CSS with CSS Custom Properties (globals.css design tokens). NO Tailwind.
techStack.auth: NextAuth v4 with CredentialsProvider (email+password) + EmailProvider (magic link via Resend) — unchanged, only re-themed
techStack.database: PostgreSQL via Drizzle ORM (node-postgres driver) — unchanged schema
techStack.reporting: DOCX generation via docx library — unchanged, re-themed trigger button only

fonts.family: Schibsted Grotesk — the ONLY typeface in the system. A humanist Scandinavian grotesk (literally designed for a Norwegian media house), used at multiple weights instead of pairing a display face with a body face.
fonts.usage: 700/600 weight for headings and metrics, 500 for labels and section titles, 400 for body copy. Tabular figures (font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums) used for all numeric data instead of switching to a monospace family.

paletteRules: Deep forest green (#1F4D3A) is the ONLY accent — used for the sidebar, primary buttons, seal rings in a good state, and active nav. It carries the whole brand.
Ochre/mustard (#C98A2B) is used exclusively for warning states — never decoratively.
Terracotta (#B4432E) is used exclusively for danger/violation states — an earthy red, not a screen-red, so it reads serious without looking alarmist.
No gradients, no glow, no drop-shadow-as-decoration. The only shadow in the system is a very faint elevation shadow reserved for modals and dropdowns.

shadows.card: none — cards are defined by a 1px solid border (#E4E0D2), not a shadow
shadows.elevated: 0 8px 24px rgba(31, 77, 58, 0.08) — reserved for modals, dropdowns, and the profile menu only

designSystem.spacing: 8px base unit, 32px page gutter (up from the current app's 24px, to reinforce the calmer, less-dense feel), 20px card padding, 16px section gap

typography.pageTitle: Schibsted Grotesk 700, 26px, normal case (not uppercase — this direction avoids shouting)
typography.label: Schibsted Grotesk 600, 11px, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.08em, textMuted — the one place uppercase is used, sparingly, for structural labels only
typography.authHeadline: Schibsted Grotesk 700, clamp(32px, 4vw, 48px), line-height 1.08 — restrained size compared to the earlier console direction, no text-shadow

designSystem.iconography: Single-weight (1.5px stroke) rounded-line icons, geometric and simple — a leaf, a drop, a checkmark, a clock. No filled icons, no gradients on icons.
designSystem.backgroundAtmosphere: None. Flat canvas color, no scanlines, no vignette, no ambient glow. Depth comes entirely from the border + generous whitespace + the one accent color, not from surface texture.

motion.pageLoad: A single, quiet staggered fade-up: each top-level panel animates opacity 0→1 and translateY(8px→0) over 320ms, 40ms stagger between panels, ease-out. No flicker, no scanline sweep, no boot sequence.
motion.sealRingFill: The ring's arc animates from 0 to its value over 700ms ease-out on mount/update — a calm fill, not a needle sweep.
motion.liveIndicator: A slow 'breathing' opacity animation (1 → 0.6 → 1 over 2.4s, ease-in-out) instead of a hard pulse or blink — reads as alive, not alarming.
motion.hover: Buttons and nav items shift background 1-2% and content translateY(-1px) over 150ms — no glow, no scale.
motion.reducedMotion: All animation collapses to a simple opacity fade or is skipped entirely.

antiPatterns: No dark-console/CRT motifs, no gauges-with-needles, no glow/scanlines — this direction is the deliberate correction away from that
No purple gradients, no generic SaaS mint/navy combo
No uppercase headlines — uppercase is reserved for the small structural label style only
No second typeface introduced for data — tabular figures within Schibsted Grotesk do that job
No mixed French/English copy — English throughout
No mock/placeholder data left visible in production — every empty state is authored and calm, not a blank div

layoutStructure.authLayout: app/(auth)/layout.tsx renders the split-screen Access layout described below, not a reused DashboardShell
layoutStructure.dashboardLayout: app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx wraps all dashboard pages in <DashboardShell>{children}</DashboardShell> — unchanged wiring, re-themed component
layoutStructure.middleware: middleware.ts uses withAuth() from next-auth/middleware — unchanged logic and exclusions

appLayout.description: Two-column grid: fixed 260px sidebar + flexible main content area. Below 980px, collapses to single column with static sidebar — unchanged from current app.
appLayout.css: .app-layout { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 260px minmax(0, 1fr); min-height: 100vh; background: var(--canvas); }

sidebar.description: Fixed 260px deep forest green sidebar — the one confident dark surface in an otherwise light system. No border-radius, no bezel treatment, just a flat fill and a soft 1px border between it and the canvas.

sections.name: Brand
sections.content: Simple leaf/drop mark (single-color, cream) + 'Milking Monitor' in Schibsted Grotesk 700 + 'Compliance Unit' subtitle in a muted sidebar text tone

styles.brandMark: 32x32px, radius 8px, background rgba(255,255,255,0.12), cream glyph, no heavy border

sections.name: Navigation
sections.iconsNote: Each item pairs with a single simple line icon (see iconography) instead of a decorative letter glyph
sections.consolidationNote: Canonical routes only — see routeConsolidation below for how /monitoring, /alerts, /recommendations, /reports, /statistics, and /sessions/new fold in

styles.navItem: min-height 42px, flex row, gap 10px, padding 10px 14px, radius 8px, color #D8E6DC, font 500 14px
styles.navItemActive: background rgba(255,255,255,0.14), color #FFFFFF, font-weight 600 — a soft filled rounded rect, not a bright pill or an edge indicator

sections.name: New Session Button
sections.styles: Cream-outlined button on the green sidebar, radius 999px, background transparent, border 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.35), fills solid cream with green text on hover — calm, not urgent-red; this app treats scheduling as routine, not an alarm action

styles.profileCard: flex row, gap 10px, padding 12px, radius 12px, background rgba(255,255,255,0.06), no border
styles.name: color #FFFFFF, font-weight 600
styles.signOut: color #D8E6DC, 12px, no underline, opacity 0.75, full opacity on hover

topbar.description: Sticky white top strip, quieter than the current app's — no backdrop blur needed since there's nothing busy behind it to blur.
topbar.css: .topbar { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 20; min-height: 76px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; gap:20px; padding:18px 32px; background: #FFFFFF; border-bottom: 1px solid #E4E0D2; }

elements.pageTitle: 26px Schibsted Grotesk 700, normal case, dynamic per route
elements.subtitle: 13px, textSecondary — kept as plain descriptive text (e.g. 'Industrial milking compliance monitoring'), no live clock gimmick
elements.search: min(30vw,320px), padding 10px 14px, radius 999px (pill, not a boxy input, to feel softer), background canvas, border 1px solid border color
elements.iconButtons: 40x40px, radius 999px, border 1px solid border color, background #FFFFFF, textSecondary icon — Notifications / Alerts / Help, same 3 as current app
elements.emergencyStop: Kept only on /live and /analytics, but restyled as a plain outlined terracotta button with the word 'Stop' — no flashing, no glow; seriousness comes from restraint, not decoration

mainContent.css: .main-shell { min-width:0; flex:1; display:flex; flex-direction:column; background: var(--canvas); }

Card.accentPanel: .accent-panel { background:#1F4D3A; color:#F7F5EF; border-color:#1F4D3A; } — used sparingly, e.g. the dashboard hero and the analytics insight panel, as the one deliberate dark moment per page

StatusTag.css: .status-tag { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:6px; padding:5px 10px; border-radius:999px; font:600 11px Schibsted Grotesk; letter-spacing:0.02em; }

Buttons.base: .button { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:8px; min-height:42px; padding:10px 16px; border-radius:8px; font:600 14px Schibsted Grotesk; transition:150ms ease; }
Buttons.primary: .button-primary { background:#1F4D3A; color:#FFFFFF; border:1px solid #1F4D3A; } hover: background #234F3C, translateY(-1px)
Buttons.secondary: .button-secondary { background:#FFFFFF; border:1px solid #E4E0D2; color:#20261F; } hover: border-color #C9C4B2
Buttons.danger: .button-danger { background:#FFFFFF; border:1px solid #B4432E; color:#B4432E; } hover: background #F5E4DF

Inputs.css: .input, .select, textarea.input { width:100%; min-height:44px; padding:11px 14px; border:1px solid #E4E0D2; border-radius:8px; background:#FFFFFF; color:#20261F; font:15px 'Schibsted Grotesk'; }
Inputs.focus: border-color #1F4D3A, box-shadow 0 0 0 3px rgba(31,77,58,0.12) — a soft focus ring, not a hard glow

Labels.css: .label { display:block; margin-bottom:8px; color:#8B968B; font:600 11px Schibsted Grotesk; letter-spacing:0.08em; text-transform:uppercase; }

DataRows.css: .check-row, .data-row { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; gap:14px; padding:14px 0; border-top:1px solid #E4E0D2; }

Metric.css: .metric { font:700 38px 'Schibsted Grotesk'; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing:-0.01em; color:#20261F; }

VideoFrame.css: .video-frame { position:relative; min-height:180px; overflow:hidden; border-radius:10px; background:#EFEDE4; border:1px solid #E4E0D2; }
VideoFrame.note: A calm flat neutral placeholder instead of the current app's striped mint-tinted pattern — texture is not needed to communicate 'this is a camera feed'

SealRing.description: Signature component. Flat SVG ring, single stroke color per state (accent for good, warning ochre for at-risk, danger terracotta for critical), no needle, no tick marks, no gradient — a clean stroke-dasharray arc that fills calmly on mount. Metric value centered inside in tabular-nums Schibsted Grotesk, small status label beneath.
SealRing.usage: Compliance score (dashboard hero, analytics), sensor/service health (equipment), missed-task rate summary (analytics), per-employee compliance (analytics table)
SealRing.replaces: The current app's conic-gradient .donut and .progress-track/.progress-fill everywhere

Table.header: .table th { color:#8B968B; font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.06em; text-align:left; text-transform:uppercase; font-weight:600; }
Table.cellPadding: .table th,.table td { padding:14px; border-bottom:1px solid #E4E0D2; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

AlertItem.css: .alert-item { padding:14px; border-radius:10px; background:#FBFAF6; border:1px solid #E4E0D2; border-left:3px solid #C98A2B; } — critical items switch the left border to #B4432E

LiveDot.css: .live-dot { width:8px; height:8px; border-radius:999px; background:#1F4D3A; animation: breathe 2.4s ease-in-out infinite; }

HeroCard.title: .hero-title { margin:0; font:700 32px 'Schibsted Grotesk'; line-height:1.1; }
HeroCard.note: Rendered on the accentPanel (deep green) treatment, cream text — the one deliberate dark hero per page, everything else on the page stays light

routeConsolidation.principle: Same audit finding as before, unchanged rationale: the current app has real duplicate pages doing the same job. One canonical route per function.

canonical./live: absorbs /monitoring's task-status grid into the same position-panel + task-row treatment used everywhere else
canonical./logs: absorbs /alerts via a severity column and filter row on the same table
canonical./analytics: absorbs /statistics (completion-rate becomes a Seal Ring at the top) and /reports (ReportGenerator + report list becomes the export panel at the bottom)
canonical./scheduler/new: the only session-creation route; app/(dashboard)/sessions/new/page.tsx becomes a 307 redirect here
canonical./sessions/[sessionId]/live: deep-link live view for a specific session; /live with no id shows the current/most-recent active session

/sign-in.name: Sign In
/sign-in.layout: Split screen, calmer proportions than the current app: minmax(400px, 1fr) minmax(420px, 1fr) — closer to even

leftPane: White background, leaf/drop brand mark + 'Milking Monitor' wordmark
H1: 'Welcome back' — 32px Schibsted Grotesk 700, sentence case (not the current app's uppercase-feeling two-line title)
Subtitle: 'Sign in to supervise compliance windows and review operator performance.' (rewritten shorter, calmer)
EmailSignInForm — pill-radius inputs matching the rest of the system, primary button 'Sign in'
Link: 'Don't have an account? Create one' to /sign-up, quiet underline

rightPane.content: Deep forest green panel (accentPanel), same /photo.png image at lower opacity (0.25) blended into the green rather than full-bleed with a dark overlay — the photo becomes texture, not the focal point
rightPane.headline: 'Every session, verified.' — 40px Schibsted Grotesk 700, cream, no text-shadow
rightPane.addition: A single small Seal Ring card floating bottom-left showing a live compliance score, quiet and small (120px), not the focal element

visualStructure.leftPane: Same brand. H1 'Create your account'. Subtitle: 'Set up a supervisor account to start monitoring compliance and generating reports.' EmailSignUpForm, button 'Create account'. Link: 'Already have an account? Sign in'
visualStructure.rightPane: Same green panel treatment, headline 'Built for the whole cooperative.'

sections.name: Hero Card
sections.left: StatusTag (Active/Standby + live-dot), session title 32px, description in #C9DAD0
sections.right: Seal Ring (compliance score) — quiet, medium-sized, cream stroke on the green panel

sections.name: Metric Row
sections.eachCard: card card-pad, .label + plain .metric number — kept as plain numbers here, not rings, since three rings in a row would be busy; rings are reserved for compliance-specific readings

left.eachPosition: card card-pad: header (position label + StatusTag), video-frame with green roi-box, 6 plain check rows (label + StatusTag) for TASK-01..06 — simple rows, no toggle-switch metaphor

right.name: Recent activity
right.content: rail-list of alert-items, 'Open Live View' secondary button linking to /live

sections.name: Main Content
items: LiveSessionBanner — accent-panel card, live-dot, elapsed/total timer in tabular-nums, session info, throughput

grid-2 of 2 position cards: video-frame + green roi-box + 6 plain task rows (StatusTag: Completed=success, Missed=danger, Unverifiable=warning, Pending=neutral) — this is where the former /monitoring tile grid folds in, using the same row treatment as everywhere else
LiveSessionStream — connection card with a small dot (success/danger) + 'Connected'/'Reconnecting' + last 8 events as plain readable rows, not a raw JSON dump styled as a terminal
TaskChecklist — same 6 tasks + Override buttons (secondary button style)

sections.name: Sidebar
sections.content: Recent Alerts (rail-list, max 6, StatusTag + time + position + 'View clip'/'Dismiss') + a small Seal Ring for compliance score at the bottom

sections.name: Header Card
sections.content: Title 'Audit history' + subtitle + filter row as simple pill toggle buttons (All / Critical / Warning) — absorbs the former /alerts view via a severity column

sections.name: Logs Table
sections.emptyState: Calm centered message: 'No unit logs have been recorded yet.' with a small outlined icon, not a blank space

sections.name: System Status
sections.content: EquipmentStatus: 4 services (Inference Service, RTSP Stream, Event Ingestion, YOLO Model) as simple rows with a StatusTag (Online/Offline/Unknown/Checking) — no gauge chips needed here, a tag is enough

sections.name: Configuration
sections.content: rail-list of data-rows: Database (PostgreSQL 16, Connected), Authentication (NextAuth.js, Active), Email Service (Resend status), Realtime Events (SSE, Active)

sections.name: Report Header
sections.content: Reporting period as a simple pill select + ReportGenerator button 'Download full report (.docx)'

sections.name: Compliance Score + Missed Tasks
sections.left: System Compliance Seal Ring (larger, signature size) + side stats: completed sessions, missed tasks, avg duration — absorbs the former /statistics completion-rate number here as the same ring
sections.right: Missed Task Frequency as a plain horizontal bar list per task type (flat single-color fill, no VU-meter segments) — simplicity over cleverness

sections.name: Efficiency + Human Factors
sections.left: Operational Efficiency as plain horizontal progress bars with a benchmark tick mark
sections.right: Human Factors table: employee, small Seal Ring chip (32px), avg speed, StatusTag

sections.name: Insight panel
sections.content: Plain, calmly-set insight copy (no teletype reveal animation) + 'Review protocol changes' secondary button in cream outline — absorbs the former /reports recent-reports list underneath as a simple list

/scheduler/new.name: Schedule new session
/scheduler/new.type: Client component (MultiStepSessionForm) — canonical route; app/(dashboard)/sessions/new/page.tsx redirects here

sections.name: Session Creation Form
sections.content: MultiStepSessionForm, 4-step wizard, step indicator as simple numbered circles connected by a thin line (current app's approach, kept as-is — it's already clean and doesn't need reinventing) with the active circle filled in accent green

sections.name: Session Info
sections.content: Session ID, employee name, StatusTag, 'Open live view' secondary button, SessionActions form

sections.name: Clinical guidance
sections.content: Absorbs /recommendations: a small risk-level StatusTag (Low/Elevated/Critical) + guidance copy, plain card, no gradient background

sections.name: Audit trail
sections.content: Cow processes count, task events count, plain list

sections.name: Sessions Table
sections.emptyState: 'No sessions scheduled yet.' calm centered message

sections.name: Supervisor Profile
sections.content: Profile card + 'Herd settings' secondary button linking to /settings/patient

sections.name: Clinical threshold
sections.content: Plain range slider (accent-green track/thumb, no potentiometer skeuomorphism) for exception sensitivity (20-60, default 32), notification status row with live-dot + 'Enabled' tag, escalation status row

/settings/patient.name: Herd settings
/settings/patient.description: Registered cow ID / RFID table with position assignment, plain table styling

DashboardShell.description: Same wiring as current (useSession(), pageTitleFunction) re-themed per sidebar/topbar specs above; pageTitleFunction updated to canonical routes only

EmailSignInForm.restyle: Standard bordered pill inputs matching the rest of the system

LiveSessionBanner.description: Same timer logic/props — visual layer only: breathing live-dot, tabular-nums timer, on the accent-panel background

LiveSessionStream.description: Same EventSource wiring — re-themed to plain readable event rows instead of a raw <pre> JSON block, to match the calmer register

TaskChecklist.description: Same 6 tasks/status set — rows become plain data-rows with StatusTag, Override as a small secondary button opening OverrideModal

OverrideModal.description: Same PATCH behavior — becomes a true centered dialog with backdrop and focus trap (fixing the current known issue), styled as a plain white card with 12px radius

SessionActions.description: Same employee reassignment PATCH — plain input styling

EquipmentStatus.description: Same 4-service check logic, re-themed to StatusTag rows; underlying no-cors reliability issue should still be fixed, see fixesFromAudit

MultiStepSessionForm.description: Same 4 steps/fields/submission — step indicator kept close to the current app's numbered-circle pattern since it already fits this calmer direction

ReportGenerator.description: Same POST /api/reports/generate — button re-themed as a plain primary button, no eject animation

/api/sessions.POST: Unchanged — 20/min, creates 2 cow_process records, notifies inference service

/api/events/ingest.POST: Unchanged — Bearer auth, 120/min, publishes to SSE broadcast

database.tables: users, accounts, auth_sessions, verification_tokens, sessions, cow_processes, task_events, reports, audit_log — schema untouched by this redesign

dataFlow.inferenceToWeb: Unchanged: Inference service -> TaskStateMachine -> POST /api/events/ingest -> ingestTaskEvent() -> publish() -> LiveSessionStream
dataFlow.override: Unchanged: Override button -> OverrideModal -> PATCH /api/task-events/[id]/override -> audit_log

responsiveDesign.behavior: Unchanged: below 980px sidebar becomes static, single-column grids, topbar stacks, search becomes full width. Seal Rings shrink to a smaller fixed diameter rather than reflowing.

fixesFromAudit: Consolidate duplicate routes per routeConsolidation above
Sanitize function (lib/security/sanitize.ts) only trims whitespace — add real HTML/XSS sanitization
Rate limiter is in-memory (Map), resets on restart — move to a shared store if deploying more than one instance
SSE broadcast is in-memory — needs a shared pub/sub layer for multi-instance production
OverrideModal is an inline card, not a true modal — implement a real dialog with backdrop and focus trap as part of this redesign
EquipmentStatus uses no-cors HEAD fetch (always opaque) — replace with a real authenticated health-check endpoint per service
Auth forms both call signIn('credentials') — wire up the existing EmailProvider magic-link flow into the UI if it's meant to be user-facing
